normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def Interval (α : Type) := { start : α // True }
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Introduce an interval type (placeholder) to model closed-open intervals.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
structure COpenInterval (α : Type) where
  start : α
  end_ : α
  (h : start ≤ end_)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define a closed-open interval [start, end) with the invariant start ≤ end.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def nonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (I J : COpenInterval α) : Prop := I.end_ ≤ J.start ∨ J.end_ ≤ I.start
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define when two intervals are mutually non-overlapping (disjoint in closed-open sense).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def pairwiseNonoverlap {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (COpenInterval α)) : Prop :=
  ∀ ⦃I⦄, I ∈ s → ∀ ⦃J⦄, J ∈ s → I ≠ J → nonoverlap I J
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define pairwise non-overlap for a set/list of intervals.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def selectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (s : List (COpenInterval α)) : Prop := pairwiseNonoverlap s
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define that a selection is valid iff its intervals are pairwise non-overlapping.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def maxSelectable {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (COpenInterval α)) : Nat :=
  Nat.findGreatest (by
    -- placeholder: existence of a maximum size among finite subsets
    admit) (by
    -- placeholder: monotonicity
    admit)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended objective: the maximum size of a selectable subset.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem maxSelectable_spec {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (COpenInterval α)) :
  (∃ s : List (COpenInterval α), s ⊆ xs ∧ selectable s ∧ s.length = maxSelectable xs) ∧
  (∀ t : List (COpenInterval α), t ⊆ xs → selectable t → t.length ≤ maxSelectable xs)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned maximum is achievable by some selectable subset, and no selectable subset is larger.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def solve {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (COpenInterval α)) : Nat := maxSelectable xs
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the algorithm result as the maximum selectable size (spec-level definition).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem solve_correct {α} [LinearOrder α] (xs : List (COpenInterval α)) :
  (∃ s : List (COpenInterval α), s ⊆ xs ∧ selectable s ∧ s.length = solve xs) ∧
  (∀ t : List (COpenInterval α), t ⊆ xs → selectable t → t.length ≤ solve xs)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The solver returns exactly the maximum number of mutually non-overlapping intervals.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem solve_nil {α} [LinearOrder α] : solve ([] : List (COpenInterval α)) = 0
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: With no intervals, the maximum selectable number is 0.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem solve_single {α} [LinearOrder α] (I : COpenInterval α) : solve [I] = 1
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: With one interval, the maximum selectable number is 1.
